llm_operation.reason: MindSpore ParallelConcat is deprecated since v2.8.0 and will be removed; official doc explicitly recommends using ops.concat() instead. TensorFlow tf.concat has fundamentally different semantics (no first-dim-size-1 constraint, supports arbitrary axis). The two ops are not semantically equivalent — ParallelConcat requires all inputs to have shape[0]==1 and concatenates only along axis 0, while tf.concat requires explicit axis and allows arbitrary dim sizes. Thus, cross-framework comparison is invalid.

llm_operation.reason: MindSpore ParallelConcat is deprecated since v2.8.0 and will be removed; official doc explicitly recommends using ops.concat instead. TensorFlow tf.concat has fundamentally different semantics (no first-dim-size-1 constraint, supports arbitrary axis).
